project.name: pqforge
project.title: pqforge: Post-Quantum Application Recipes
project.tagline: Post-quantum file, folder, text, media, webhook, token, document, and server recipes for Dart.
project.summary: pqforge turns pqcrypto ML-KEM and ML-DSA primitives into application-ready envelopes, signatures, hybrid key agreement, wrapped key custody, and a local/server CLI.

capabilities: X25519 plus ML-KEM built-in hybrid key agreement
ML-DSA plus Ed25519 built-in hybrid signatures
Argon2id plus AES-GCM wrapped key custody
Named recipes for files, folders, text, media, email, documents, webhooks, tokens, records, logs, artifacts, and identity bindings
Universal CLI for local and server encryption, decryption, signing, and verification
does_not_provide: A hard constant-time guarantee for Dart runtimes
Public-key trust, identity vetting, or legal e-signature policy
Replay stores, authorization policy, TLS, or secure infrastructure operations

allowed_claims: Application-layer composition helpers for KEM-DEM, AEAD sessions, wrapped key custody, signatures, recipes, and CLI workflows.
evidence_boundary.summary: The package provides application composition around pqcrypto evidence. It is not a validated cryptographic module.

profiles.name: compact
profiles.use: smaller demos, constrained payloads, and low-bandwidth signatures

profiles.name: maximum
profiles.use: long-lived records, archives, media, and high-value artifacts

recipes.name: Local File Vault
recipes.apps: developer machines, backup jobs, admin laptops, server batch jobs
recipes.caveat: Use wrapped secret keys and external storage policy.

recipes.name: Folder Archive
recipes.apps: case files, release bundles, evidence folders, internal archives
recipes.caveat: Each file is an independent envelope; design streaming for very large files.

recipes.name: Text Snippets
recipes.apps: notes, secrets, prompts, policy snippets, short messages
recipes.caveat: UTF-8 text only; binary data should use file or media recipes.

recipes.name: Media Assets
recipes.apps: photos, audio, video, PDFs, campaign media, identity images
recipes.caveat: Bind MIME type and media id; classify content before storing.

recipes.name: Document Signing
recipes.apps: contracts, reports, certificates, approvals, policy documents
recipes.caveat: Application owns legal policy and signer identity vetting.

recipes.name: Webhook Integrity
recipes.apps: payment callbacks, audit delivery, server-to-server events
recipes.caveat: Application owns replay windows and key publication.

recipes.name: Signed Tokens
recipes.apps: API capabilities, admin actions, short-lived grants
recipes.caveat: Application owns revocation and authorization policy.

recipes.name: Private Email Payloads
recipes.apps: encrypted mail bodies, secure notifications, outbound archive mail
recipes.caveat: Transport headers and metadata may remain visible unless the app hides them.

recipes.name: Government And Medical Records
recipes.apps: public-sector records, patient records, registry payloads
recipes.caveat: Application owns access control, retention, and audit policy.

recipes.name: Software Artifacts
recipes.apps: release bundles, firmware, packages, configuration rollouts
recipes.caveat: Sign version and target metadata to block rollback replay.

recipes.name: Hybrid Server Sessions
recipes.apps: Serverpod, API services, local agents, backend handshakes
recipes.caveat: Authenticate public key bundles and design replay/session storage.

faq.question: Where does the CLI store keys?
faq.answer: In the directory passed to keygen --out-dir. Public keys are raw JSON. Secret keys are wrapped JSON when a passphrase source is supplied.

faq.question: Are CLI keys password protected?
faq.answer: Yes when keygen is run with --passphrase-env, --passphrase-file, or --passphrase. Wrapped secrets use Argon2id plus AES-256-GCM.

faq.question: Does pqforge support RC4?
faq.answer: No. RC4 is not PQC, not AEAD, and not safe for new systems.

faq.question: Can AES sign documents?
faq.answer: No. AES encrypts; ML-DSA or hybrid signatures sign and verify.

faq.question: Which apps can use pqforge?
faq.answer: Local file vaults, folder archives, server jobs, webhook systems, token issuers, document workflows, media archives, email payload protection, government/medical record systems, signed release pipelines, and hybrid server sessions.
